right_target.finding: OpenTargets returns 764 evidence rows for melanoma; ERBB2 appears only via cancer_gene_census somatic_mutation entries (score 1.0) for cutaneous, nodular, lentigo maligna and superficial spreading melanoma plus one europePMC literature row (resourceScore 138), reflecting the general 'cancer gene' census rather than a melanoma-driver role. GWAS returns just 3 SNP records for ERBB2 (rs14050 3'UTR x2, rs147005258 intron) with no melanoma trait association. Causal genetic support in melanoma is weak.
right_target.provenance: OpenTargets_get_evidence_by_datasource(gene=ERBB2,disease=melanoma): 764 rows, cancer_gene_census somatic_mutation score=1.0, europepmc resourceScore=138; gwas_get_snps_for_gene(ERBB2): 3 SNPs, no melanoma trait

right_tissue.finding: CellMarker returns 25 human records for ERBB2. Cancer-cell expression is concentrated in Breast (supports=4), Bladder and Blood; there is NO melanoma/melanocyte or skin cancer-cell entry. ERBB2 is not a melanoma-tissue marker.
right_tissue.provenance: CellMarker_search_by_gene(search_by_gene,ERBB2,Human): 25 records; top cancer-cell tissue Breast supports=4; no melanoma/skin cancer-cell record

right_safety.finding: OpenFDA reports 36,591 adverse-event reports for trastuzumab (HER2 antagonist; known cardiotoxicity class). CellMarker normal-cell ERBB2 expression is broad: CD8+ T cell and beta cell (Blood), mesenchymal stem cells (Adipose/Bone/Cartilage/Muscle/Tendon), radial glial cell (Brain), and epithelial cells (cervix, undefined). Broad normal expression plus a large real-world AE burden signals meaningful on-target safety liability.
right_safety.provenance: OpenFDA_search_drug_events(trastuzumab): total=36591; CellMarker normal cells: CD8+ T cell, beta cell, mesenchymal stem cell (multi-tissue), radial glial cell, epithelial cell

right_patient.finding: Only 3 clinical trials match ERBB2/trastuzumab in melanoma, and none is a melanoma-selective HER2 program: NCT04639219 (T-DXd) is a pan-tumor HER2-mutation basket that merely lists melanoma among ~11 histologies; NCT02308020 (abemaciclib, CDK4/6) and NCT02983045 (NKTR-214/nivolumab) are not ERBB2-directed. No HER2-selected melanoma patient population exists.
right_patient.provenance: search_clinical_trials(intervention=ERBB2 OR trastuzumab,condition=melanoma): total_count=3; NCT04639219 HER2-mutation basket, NCT02308020 CDK4/6, NCT02983045 IL-2/anti-PD1

right_commercial.finding: TheraSAbDab lists 20 anti-ERBB2 therapeutics including multiple Approved agents (trastuzumab, pertuzumab, margetuximab, disitamab ADC, zanidatamab, zenocutuzumab) and several Phase-II/III assets. ERBB2 is a heavily validated, commercially mature target — but the entire franchise is breast/gastric/solid-tumor HER2+, with no melanoma indication, so competitive whitespace in melanoma specifically is unproven.
right_commercial.provenance: TheraSAbDab_search_by_target(ERBB2): count=20, incl. Approved trastuzumab/pertuzumab/margetuximab/disitamab/zanidatamab/zenocutuzumab; none melanoma-indicated

tractability.finding: Pharos classifies ERBB2 (P04626) as Tclin (highest tractability tier: an approved-drug target), family Kinase, with 3,216 publications and multiple marketed antibodies and small molecules. Biophysically and clinically ERBB2 is maximally tractable.
